task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenC3 COSMOS dashboard or interface is visible in the web browser.

task_completion.description: The Script Runner tool is opened to automate the procedure
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenC3 Script Runner window or tab is visible.

task_completion.description: Script contains logic to sample telemetry 3 times at 1-second intervals and calculate the mean
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code in Script Runner showing repeated telemetry calls (e.g., tlm('INST HEALTH_STATUS TEMP1')) with 1-second delays (e.g., wait(1) or sleep(1)) and a mathematical mean calculation.

task_completion.description: Script contains conditional logic for the temperature threshold and corresponding commands
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code showing an 'if' statement checking if the mean is < 55.0 to send 'INST COLLECT' (with TYPE: NORMAL, DURATION: 5) and an 'else' or >= 55.0 condition to send 'INST ABORT'.
Script Runner execution logs showing the script running, telemetry being read, and a command being sent.

task_completion.description: The required JSON report is created with the correct keys
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/conditional_pass_report.json' containing exactly the keys: 'samples', 'mean_temperature', 'branch_executed', and 'command_sent'.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent interacted with OpenC3 COSMOS to execute the script rather than just writing it
integrity.visual_evidence: Execution logs in Script Runner or command counters incrementing in the OpenC3 interface, indicating real interaction.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent automated the procedure as requested rather than manually sending commands
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of a script in Script Runner that performs the logic, rather than the agent just using the manual Command Sender tool.

integrity.description: Verifies the JSON report contains realistic data from the execution
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'samples' and 'mean_temperature' in the JSON report look like actual numerical float values derived from the script execution, not hardcoded placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target: INST. Temperature threshold: 55.0. Nominal command: COLLECT. Contingency command: ABORT. Output file: /home/ga/Desktop/conditional_pass_report.json.
